items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement spending plans
items.summary: Mountain View is laying out major infrastructure and facility projects for the upcoming fiscal year. Residents can learn what physical improvements are being prioritized for streets, parks, utilities, and public buildings.

items.headline: City considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing an ordinance to amend its Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations for residents throughout the city.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan moves forward
items.summary: City Council reviewed the status of the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. This plan addresses improvements to San José's library system and services.

items.headline: Downtown historic building seeks density bonus for renovation
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is seeking to be elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area in exchange for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This decision could reshape a downtown property while preserving its historic character.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial to a planned community, with plans for an eight-story building containing 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This is a significant land-use change affecting a major corridor in Palo Alto.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been cancelled and rescheduled for April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street will not be heard on the original date and will receive a new hearing notice when rescheduled.

items.headline: City Council Approves Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda document does not specify the project scope or location. Residents should monitor future updates for details on what infrastructure or development work this contract covers.

items.headline: Grant awarded for School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.
